# AssistantCaddy Rollout Ledger - 2026-06-05

This is the working checklist for rolling today's feedback into the current ThreatCaddy standalone.

Standalone target:

- `/Users/brdavies/Documents/ThreatCaddy updates/threatcaddy-standalone.html`

Source app:

- `/Users/brdavies/Documents/ThreatCaddy updates/ThreatCaddy-V3`

Odysseus reference:

- `/Users/brdavies/odysseus`

## Release Rules

- Preserve ThreatCaddy's master theme and do not clone Odysseus visually wholesale.
- Use Odysseus as the interaction reference for windows, settings, email drafting, calendar density, and animated backgrounds.
- Keep domain/provider language agnostic. Microsoft, Google, Proton, Zoom, Teams, Meet, Webex, Slack, and maps should be examples, not hard-coded assumptions.
- CaddyAI can analyze, summarize, sanitize, and draft. It must not directly send email.
- ThreatCaddy may stage platform send actions only after explicit human confirmation.
- Use external app/link launches for meetings, maps, and provider account flows.
- Keep case/investigation substance out of global memory. Store only reusable method/process lessons.
- Before risky UI edits, multi-agent merges, or `pnpm update:standalone`, preserve a dated source snapshot of high-risk touched files and confirm line counts/expected exports. Do not promote a standalone rebuild from a damaged or suspicious source tree.
- If a touched source file is truncated or malformed, freeze standalone promotion, restore from git/snapshot/session logs/known-good artifact, then rerun typecheck and focused tests before rebuilding.
- Current standalone static smoke should use one URL: `http://127.0.0.1:4181/threatcaddy-standalone.html`. Do not start extra ad hoc standalone smoke ports unless `4181` is occupied or blocked; document the conflict and stop the fallback server when done. `127.0.0.1:4179` had a stale unhealthy listener during the 2026-06-09 audit and was stopped; do not reuse it for this rollout unless `4181` is blocked and the fallback is documented. `localhost:4173` / `127.0.0.1:4173` is the Playwright/Vite app test server, not the standalone static-smoke port.
- During multi-agent SecDevOps rollout work, keep one read-only agent manager assigned. The manager should track idle, running, and blocked agents; recycle idle agents into bounded non-overlapping reviews or worker tasks after each completed task; and keep required gates visible. The integrator remains responsible for final edits, promotion decisions, and accepting, rejecting, or deferring raw agent findings.

## Information Architecture

Acceptance criteria:

- Sidebar order is `Dashboard`, `CaddyShack`, `Investigations`, `AssistantCaddy`, `FortuneINT`, `Team Feed`, `AgentCaddy`.
- `Investigations` expands to `CaddyAI`, `Notes`, `Tasks`, `Evidence`, `Products`, `Timeline`, `Whiteboards`, `IOCs`, `Graph`, `Activity`.
- `AssistantCaddy` is both a real overview panel and a dropdown parent.
- `AssistantCaddy` expands to `EmailCaddy` and `CalendarCaddy`.
- Visible naming uses `AssistantCaddy`, `EmailCaddy`, and `CalendarCaddy`.
- Legacy visible strings such as `CaddyAssistant`, `CadEmail`, `CadEmail workspace`, `CalendarCaddy workspace`, `Odyssey Appearance Lab`, and `Scheduler Lane` are removed unless hidden code aliases are required for compatibility.
- `CaddyShack` is the former Experimental route and should not show the old experimental layout that conflicted with the user-approved direction.

## Appearance, Themes, And Color Harmony

Top priority because the user explicitly asked that this review sit above the rest.

Acceptance criteria:

- Settings should keep ThreatCaddy's original top-tab layout instead of the newer Odysseus-style left settings rail.
- Settings tabs should remain compact text-first tabs across the top: `General`, `Appearance`, `AI`, `Agents`, `Data`, `Templates`, `Intel`, `Integrations`, `Shortcuts`, and `System` where space allows.
- The newer Appearance content can remain, but it must sit inside the original ThreatCaddy settings shell style.
- Appearance settings show distinct `ThreatCaddy themes` and `Odysseus themes` sections.
- Odysseus theme presets are imported from the real Odysseus palette table or a documented equivalent mapping, not just inferred from screenshots.
- The Odysseus section is named as imported presets, not `Odyssey Appearance Lab`.
- Sidebar colored dropdown icons remain toggleable from Appearance settings.
- Dropdown and submenu highlights should reuse the burger/sidebar active glow language where possible, so filter/menu selections feel like the same ThreatCaddy element family rather than separate native controls.
- Color Harmony behaves like Odysseus: choose accent color, choose harmony type, choose mode, preview the generated palette, then apply/generate as a full palette.
- ThreatCaddy may still expose expandable field-level assignments, but those are secondary to the Odysseus-style generator flow.
- Harmony options include at least `Complementary`, `Analogous`, `Triadic`, and `Monochromatic`.
- Mode is explicit (`dark` / `light`) and affects generated background, panel, text, border, and accent colors.
- Native select/dropdown popups should inherit ThreatCaddy theme tokens instead of falling back to white OS/browser menus on dark or green themes.
- Native select/dropdown text and selected rows must remain readable when the app flips between dark and light theme modes.
- The Appearance preview should represent accent coloring accurately enough to catch logo/title/nav contrast issues, including ThreatCaddy-style two-tone logo text.
- Preview click-to-color should map background/panel/element regions accurately; clicking a panel background should open the relevant background or panel color field instead of requiring a precise text/button click.
- The color dropper action should select the intended preview UI region and should not drift to unrelated accent/navigation fields.
- Color picker eyedropper/dropper buttons must work consistently across Appearance palette fields, Odysseus/theme palette options, harmony accent controls, and background/effect color controls. If the browser or standalone context does not expose the native eyedropper API, the button should be disabled or show a clear fallback instead of appearing clickable but doing nothing.
- Background motion is actually visible in the main app when enabled.
- Background effects include Odysseus-style options where feasible: synapse, rain, constellations, perlin flow, petals, sparkles, embers.
- Odysseus theme presets should preserve the real Odysseus prebaked background pairings: `dark` none, `light` dots, `midnight` rain, `paper` dots, `cyberpunk` synapse, `retrowave` embers, `forest` petals, `ocean` constellations, `terminal` perlin-flow, `organs` rain, `ume` petals, `cute` sparkles, with matching effect-color/intensity/frosted defaults where Odysseus defines them.
- Appearance should add a compact background-animation bubble picker so users can handpick a theme and then swap its animation independently.
- When a user swaps a background animation, ThreatCaddy should theme the effect color appropriately from the active palette while still allowing manual override.
- ThreatCaddy themes and Odysseus themes should both support animation swapping; Odysseus presets simply start with their prebaked animation defaults.
- Background image layering and frosted panel settings do not hide the canvas unintentionally.
- Reduced-motion users still get a safe non-jarring experience.

## CalendarCaddy

Acceptance criteria:

- CalendarCaddy opens as a real calendar workspace, not a generic card panel.
- Month view fits on a normal laptop screen without needing unusable internal scrollbars when empty or lightly populated.
- Week view has hourly rows with 15-minute or 30-minute usable snap increments.
- Week view can scroll the day timeline while keeping header context visible.
- Sunday is the start of the week.
- Today's date is visually highlighted.
- The current day is stated somewhere visible in the calendar shell.
- Month/date/today controls and week/month/year controls live in the top row.
- Settings and refresh/sync buttons are inline on the same row when space allows.
- The assistant text field is compact and sits near the top, with the AI/audio/action icon on the right.
- Stamp controls sit beside the assistant prompt to save vertical space.
- Stamp buttons rely on iconography and hover text, not full text labels.
- Selecting the same stamp toggles stamp mode off.
- Used stamps glow/highlight in the stamp bank for the selected day or selected event.
- Clicking a stamp badge on a day removes that stamp.
- Clicking a stamp badge on an event removes that explicit event stamp.
- A day supports up to three stamps with slight visual separation.
- Multi-stamp days use compact top-row badges plus outline/ring treatment without destroying monthly event readability.
- Event cards inherit explicit event stamp outlines.
- Date-level stamps can be dragged across multiple days, including PTO-style ranges.
- Stamp set includes birthday, holiday, vacation/PTO, school, travel, focus/work, family, medical, deadline, and parental leave.
- Month cells allow inserting or dragging to create all-day/ranged events.
- Week cells allow click-to-place and drag-to-size a provisional `New event`.
- Releasing a week drag leaves the provisional `New event` on the grid instead of immediately opening the drawer.
- Single click selects an event.
- Double click opens create/edit drawer.
- Right click opens contextual options.
- A selected event can be removed with the keyboard Delete key when focus is not inside an input, textarea, select, contenteditable field, or active editor drawer.
- Keyboard deletion should clear selected-event state, remove event-specific stamps, close any event context menu, and show a clear removed-event status message.
- Events show visible top/bottom resize handles.
- Vertical resizing is smooth at 15-minute increments, not sticky to hour lines.
- Horizontal drag moves an event to adjacent days while preserving the original time.
- Horizontal selection across adjacent days works in week view.
- Month drag selection works for event creation and stamp ranges.
- Event create/edit drawer supports title, dates, times, source, conference app/link, repeat, count, location/handoff, notes, delete, cancel, and save.
- Keyboard support should match normal calendar expectations: Enter/Return activates the selected option or confirms the active appointment form when focus is on an action-safe control.
- Tab and Shift+Tab should move through appointment fields, toolbar actions, stamp controls, and email controls in a predictable visible-focus order.
- Number-row and numpad input should work naturally for time/date fields, including quick entry of appointment start/end times.
- CalendarCaddy should avoid hijacking Enter/Delete/Backspace while focus is inside editable fields.
- Test data should cover a normal appointment, a Zoom appointment, a recurring daily appointment for three weeks, and a two-week August PTO all-day block.

## EmailCaddy

Acceptance criteria:

- EmailCaddy reads like a dense mail client, not a collection of oversized cards.
- The layout is rowed, compact, and comparable to Outlook/Odysseus density while keeping ThreatCaddy visual identity.
- Toolbar dropdowns should use ThreatCaddy-shaped rounded popovers instead of native square OS menus where that visual mismatch is distracting.
- The selected email/draft reader should not be trapped below an oversized inbox area; the inbox/reader split should have a draggable horizontal resize handle.
- The selected email/draft reader content should scroll independently so the body, draft fields, safety notes, and action buttons remain reachable.
- Bulk selection works by row checkbox and by type/filter.
- Bulk controls are compact dropdowns or toolbar controls, not large permanent panels.
- Compose, reply, reply-all, forward, CC, BCC, subject, and body fields exist and are editable.
- Drafting mirrors Odysseus' document-backed flow conceptually: the user stages/edits drafts, and AI does not send.
- Forward/reply modes preserve useful quoted context and attachment chips.
- `Sanitize`, `What am I forgetting?`, sensitivity warning, classification tags, audience-depth guess, and ask extraction are available as assistant aids.
- Important/sender-driven email alert logic is planned for one-to-one or low-recipient-count messages.
- Sidebar/burger menu can show an unread/new-mail badge once mail state is lifted out of the workspace.
- External provider onboarding remains agnostic and does not assume a single corporate domain.
- EmailCaddy should support expected mail-client keyboard behavior where safe: Enter/Return opens or activates the selected row/action, Tab/Shift+Tab traverse fields and toolbar controls, Escape exits transient menus/drawers, and Delete/Backspace only delete selected mail when focus is not inside a draft field.

## AssistantCaddy

Acceptance criteria:

- AssistantCaddy is cleaner than the card-heavy prototype.
- Widgets are optional panel/settings modules, not the main panel layout.
- AssistantCaddy offers setup guidance when no AI provider is configured.
- AssistantCaddy offers setup guidance when no email/calendar account is configured.
- Onboarding can guide Microsoft, Google, Proton, Zoom/Teams/Meet/Webex, maps, and similar providers without hard-coding a specific employer.
- AssistantCaddy can route the user to EmailCaddy, CalendarCaddy, setup, daily brief, prep, sanitization, or "what am I forgetting" flows.
- AssistantCaddy can suggest stamping/tagging based on recognizable calendar/email content such as birthdays or PTO, but it asks before applying.
- AssistantCaddy should be able to turn commitments found in email into calendar holds/events, then optionally sync/stage them across calendars after confirmation.

## Desktop Workspace, Windows, And Notifications

Phase two unless a minimal shell can be safely added in this pass.

Acceptance criteria:

- ThreatCaddy should evolve toward a desktop/workspace model with draggable, minimizable, resizable, and snappable panes.
- Odysseus references to port conceptually: `modalManager`, `windowDrag`, `modalSnap`, `tileManager`, draggable minimized dock chips, edge docking, and ghost snap previews.
- Workspace panes should behave like pullable in-app tabs: a user can pull a note, task, email, calendar, CaddyAI session, or investigation tool into shared screen space, work with it there, then close it back to its source location without losing state.
- Closing a popped-out pane should return it to where it came from while preserving edits, draft text, task state, note content, selected investigation context, and active assistant/session state.
- The workspace model should allow mixed surfaces at the same time, such as EmailCaddy beside CaddyAI running commands, notes from a specific investigation, task lists, and CalendarCaddy.
- Floating or snapped panes should be available across AssistantCaddy, CaddyAI, Notes, Tasks, CalendarCaddy, EmailCaddy, and other major workspace sections rather than being bespoke to one popup.
- Smart minimization should hide secondary controls, settings, stamps, filters, and helper chrome when a pane is shrunk, while leaving the main tool exposed and usable, such as the note editor, primary calendar grid, email draft, or task list.
- Odysseus is the near-term interaction reference for movable windows, but ThreatCaddy should extend the model with stronger snapping, resizing, state preservation, and workspace composition.
- Pane visual treatment, resize affordances, shadows, drag headers, glow language, and theme contrast should become a uniform ThreatCaddy ruleset for all panes, whether they are popped out, docked, snapped, or sitting in their normal flat frame.
- Resizable sections should expose subtle edge-hover affordances, such as a glow near pane borders that tells the user the section can be resized without permanently adding more chrome.
- Resize affordances should exist on top, right, bottom, and left edges, with all four corners acting as diagonal resizers.
- When hovering a resizable edge, a small centered bar should appear parallel to that edge and slightly inset from the panel edge, with a subtle glow to show the pull direction.
- The hover glow should wrap continuously around the full pane border/corners rather than stopping at the active corner. The brighter resize indicator is separate from that border glow and must sit just outside the panel by roughly one line-width: outside-left for left, outside-right for right, outside-top for top, and outside-bottom for bottom. Edge indicator bars should run parallel to the active side and span roughly 66-75% of that side length. Corner hover should use a compact bent or curved accent indicator just outside the hovered corner so the diagonal resize target reads as a corner pull, not as two unrelated edge highlights.
- Floating-pane shadows should be tight and directional, biased to the right and bottom only; avoid broad halo shadows on the top and left.
- Floating pane headers should be the drag handle. Do not add a fake draggable pill, label, or button inside the header when the header itself can move the panel.
- Pane/context surfaces should support right-click or compact menu actions for `pop out`, `dock back into UI`, and snap targets.
- Snap targets should include top half, bottom half, left side, and right side, with a visible preview before the panel commits to that placement.
- CalendarCaddy can be minimized to a compact calendar-only surface when the panel is small.
- EmailCaddy can remain closer to a page/workspace initially, but should support notifications and unread badges.
- Bottom-right notification bubbles do not stack.
- Important email bubbles include sender/context and close/action options.
- Meeting bubbles appear before meeting time, include meeting title/time and `Join [app] Meeting`, and open the external link/app.
- Meeting bubbles can pulse/glow/jiggle at start time, with reduced-motion/preference controls.
- Slack or chat-style integrations can later alert for one-to-one or small-group DMs without over-alerting noisy channels.

## Testing And Release Gates

Acceptance criteria:

- Run TypeScript validation after patches.
- Run focused Vitest files for settings, sidebar, and AssistantCaddy workspaces.
- Rebuild the standalone with `pnpm update:standalone`.
- Smoke-test the file URL target in the in-app browser.
- Verify Appearance: Odysseus themes, Color Harmony flow, sidebar icon toggle, background motion visibility.
- Verify CalendarCaddy: Sunday start, today highlight, prompt/stamps placement, stamp add/remove/range, week drag vertical/horizontal, month drag, event edit drawer.
- Verify EmailCaddy: search/filter, bulk selection, compose, reply, reply all, forward, CC/BCC, sanitize, coverage check, staged send guardrail.
- Verify AssistantCaddy: setup prompts for missing AI/email/calendar, route buttons, and clean layout.
- Record residual gaps plainly as `DONE`, `PARTIAL`, or `DEFERRED`.
- During long-running rollout work, create an hourly rollback/handoff checkpoint with snapshots, completed goals, unresolved issues, current gate status, artifact status, and the next rollback point. Agent notes can be appended as task logs, but the integrator must review them before marking them verified.

## Agent Lane Plan

- Planner/integrator: owns this ledger, merge order, standalone rebuild, and final status.
- Critique auditor: checks this ledger against today's user feedback and identifies missed acceptance criteria.
- Odysseus parity auditor: compares real Odysseus theme, background, email, calendar, and window behavior against ThreatCaddy.
- Developer lane 1: Appearance and background parity.
- Developer lane 2: CalendarCaddy interaction polish.
- Developer lane 3: EmailCaddy usability polish.
- Tester lane 1: focused automated tests and file URL smoke.
- Tester lane 2: manual UI flow checklist with fake email/calendar data.
- Auditor lane: verifies edits against the ledger and rejects missed or over-broad changes.

## Current Priority Order

1. Color Harmony parity and background visibility.
2. CalendarCaddy drag/stamp/event creation polish.
3. EmailCaddy compose/reply/forward/bulk usability polish.
4. AssistantCaddy setup/onboarding cleanup.
5. Standalone rebuild and focused UI smoke.
6. Window manager/notification architecture as phase two if not safely shippable in this pass.

### Agent Feedback Log - 2026-06-06

- SecDevOps agent: enforce snapshots before risky edits and before standalone promotion; freeze promotion on truncation or malformed source; verify source gates, focused tests, artifact parity, and browser smoke; avoid confusing dev-server storage with `file://` standalone storage.
- UX/test agent: first missing test contract was shared panel frame behavior. Required accessible controls include popout, dock, minimize, restore, named edge/corner resize handles, snap preview status, and custom combobox/listbox/options. EmailCaddy dropdown popovers should remain `PARTIAL` until source actually implements them, now addressed for the toolbar controls.
- Implementation-risk agent: first slice should be EmailCaddy only. Avoid `App.tsx` and `Sidebar.tsx` initially because they are merge-conflict magnets and view-key risk points. CalendarCaddy should be second because it has drag/drop, resize, context menus, keyboard handlers, and localStorage state.
- Architecture agent: Odysseus should be ported conceptually, not copied directly. The long-term shape should be a React `WorkspacePanelProvider` with reducer-owned panel lifecycle state, preserved mount policies, z-order, pre-snap/pre-dock geometry, drag/resize hooks, snap preview, and a dock layer. Avoid Odysseus-style global body class and MutationObserver layout mutations in ThreatCaddy.

## Planning Addition - Investigation Context Chrome

Status: `PLANNED` for a later desktop-workspace phase. Do not implement in the current panel-consumer slice.

- `PLANNED`: Add shared investigation context chrome for the desktop workspace. The intended UI is a ThreatCaddy-styled header/footer treatment using the same outline/glow language as the panel borders, with clear TLP/classification status visible while workspace panels are open.
- `PLANNED`: Add a compact active-investigation bar under the top chrome. It should show the currently active investigation/case, expose buttons for case details/metadata, and provide an investigation dropdown for switching cases without leaving the desktop workspace.
- `PLANNED`: Add a simple right-click/context menu on `Investigations` and the active-investigation surface to choose an investigation by name. Keep the first version minimal: a ThreatCaddy-styled popover/list with investigation names, current investigation highlighted, and no heavy details panel.
- `UX EXPECTATION`: The right-click investigation chooser should be fast and low-noise. It should open near the cursor or trigger, support click-outside/Escape dismissal, preserve keyboard access through an equivalent button/menu path, and select the chosen investigation without changing the current workspace layout.
- `SECDEVOPS`: The chooser should read from the typed investigation list/state, not scrape visible DOM text. Do not expose note contents, task details, IOC values, evidence text, prompts, or other case substance in the menu; names and minimal status affordances are enough for the first version.
- `PLANNED`: Investigation switching should update investigation-scoped panels together: Notes, Tasks, CaddyAI/CaddyAssistant context, IOCs, Evidence, Timeline, Products, and any future investigation-bound panel should re-source from the newly selected investigation.
- `PLANNED`: Investigation switching must not mutate the workspace layout/template. Panel positions, snapped/docked/floating state, z-order, and saved workspace templates remain presentation state; the active investigation is runtime context, not part of the layout template unless a future import/export flow explicitly stores an optional, recoverable case reference.
- `PLANNED`: The active-investigation bar should make cross-case context obvious so analysts can keep Notes, Tasks, and Investigations open without silently mixing entities from different cases.
- `SECDEVOPS`: Treat TLP/classification and active-investigation context as a safety boundary. Switching investigations should clear or validate selected entity ids that do not belong to the new investigation, preserve screenshare/classification filters, and prevent stale panels from displaying prior-case content after the active case changes.
- `SECDEVOPS`: Layout template save/import/export must remain content-free. Do not store note text, task descriptions, email bodies, IOC values, prompts, tokens, file payloads, or secrets in workspace templates. If templates later carry an optional investigation reference, validate it and degrade gracefully when the referenced investigation is missing.
- `PLANNED`: Add assistant stale-case nudges after metadata support exists. AssistantCaddy should not need to read private investigation contents to do this; it can use bounded metadata such as status, last-updated time, open task counts, and stale age to ask whether a case should be reviewed, closed, archived, or kept active.
- `SECDEVOPS`: Stale-case assistance must be confirm-before-action. The assistant may ask or draft a closeout/checklist, but it must not close, archive, delete, or change an investigation without an explicit user action.
- `TESTING`: Future coverage should prove that selecting a different investigation updates all investigation-scoped panels, leaves workspace geometry/templates unchanged, updates the TLP/status chrome, clears invalid selected entity ids, and does not leak prior-case data into the newly selected case.
- `TESTING`: Add focused coverage for the right-click investigation chooser: context menu opens from `Investigations`, lists investigation names, highlights or labels the active investigation, switches active investigation on selection, supports Escape/click-outside dismissal, has a keyboard-accessible equivalent, and does not mutate workspace layout/template state.

## Rollout Addition - 2026-06-07 08:54 EDT - First-Class Workspace Entry And Drag-To-Workspace Launch

Status: `PARTIAL`. The first-class `Workspace` sidebar route was implemented in the 2026-06-07 09:30 EDT Phase 8 slice below. Drag-to-workspace launch, keyboard `Open in Workspace` commands, and persisted layout/template behavior remain planned future work.

- `GOAL`: Add a first-class `Workspace` sidebar/burger tab item near the existing high-level items such as `CaddyShack` and `Investigations`. This gives users an obvious way to return to the custom desktop/workspace area after selecting other panels or route views.
- `GOAL`: Selecting ordinary side-menu items should be able to jump to, focus, or create that item's viewing panel, while selecting `Workspace` should return to the custom freeform workspace layout without forcing a specific app panel to become active.
- `GOAL`: Add a direct drag-from-menu launch path. A user should be able to drag a sidebar/burger menu item or tab itself into the workspace area and drop it there to pop out/create that panel at or near the drop location, instead of first navigating into the panel, clicking `Pop out`, then moving it.
- `UX EXPECTATION`: Dragging a menu item should show a workspace-compatible drag preview and a valid drop target. On drop, the UI should attempt to fit the selected surface into the workspace using existing panel geometry rules, snap constraints, viewport-safe bounds, z-order focus, and restore semantics.
- `UX EXPECTATION`: Menu-item drag should not replace normal click navigation. Click still navigates/focuses; drag creates or moves a workspace panel. Keyboard and accessible alternatives should exist for users who cannot drag.
- `RUNTIME EXPECTATION`: Drag-to-workspace should operate on safe panel descriptors: panel id, source route/view id, desired mode, geometry/drop point, and restore target. It should not serialize or drag note/email/task contents, prompts, secrets, tokens, file payloads, or investigation data.
- `RUNTIME EXPECTATION`: The workspace route should become a stable home for custom arrangements, paired panels, and future layout templates. It should be able to show no active panel, several floating panels, minimized dock chips, and restored saved layouts without being tied to a single feature route.
- `SECDEVOPS GUARDRAILS`: Validate all dragged panel ids against a static allowlist/registry. Ignore or reject unknown panel ids, executable fields, offscreen geometry, invalid route targets, or unsupported drop payloads. Clamp dropped geometry to safe viewport bounds.
- `SECDEVOPS GUARDRAILS`: Dragging a menu item into the workspace must not trigger side effects such as CaddyAI sends, AgentCaddy runs, local bridge calls, imports, uploads, deletes, approvals, deploys, or external navigation. First tests should be shell-only/no-run for high-risk surfaces.
- `TESTING EXPECTATION`: Add focused unit coverage for `Workspace` navigation, panel registry lookup, drag payload validation, geometry clamping, and click-vs-drag separation. Add browser smoke for dragging at least one low-risk menu item into the workspace, then minimizing/restoring it from the dock.
- `ACCESSIBILITY EXPECTATION`: Add keyboard-accessible commands such as `Open in Workspace` / `Pop out to Workspace` from each eligible menu item, plus visible focus state and screen-reader status for valid drop, invalid drop, and panel-created events.
- `DEFERRED`: This planning item does not yet implement persisted workspace layouts, workspace template save/import/export, app/sidebar minimized integration, or deep smart-minimization behavior. Those remain adjacent future phases.

## Rollout Addition - 2026-06-07 08:57 EDT - Investigation-Scoped CaddyAI Quick Button

Status: `PLANNED` for future investigation/workspace UX and CaddyAI integration. Not implemented in this ledger update.

- `GOAL`: Add a small CaddyAI/AI button to the investigation tab/dropdown area so an analyst can summon an investigation-scoped CaddyAI helper without opening a full CaddyAI workspace window.
- `GOAL`: The helper should launch as a compact popout, flyout, or mini panel that stays near the active investigation context and can remain available while the main workspace is dense, minimized, or showing fewer toolbar controls because of custom fit rules.
- `GOAL`: The helper should understand the currently selected investigation context and be able to pivot to the correct notes, tasks, IOCs, timeline, evidence, and related CaddyAI context using existing read/write capabilities where already supported by CaddyAI.
- `GOAL`: The helper should assist with small analyst actions such as finding relevant case notes, opening the right investigation panel, drafting or adding tasks, summarizing the active investigation state, finding stale or missing follow-ups, or swapping visible investigation-backed panels to the active case.
- `UX EXPECTATION`: The button should be lightweight and always easy to reach from the investigation selector/dropdown, especially when the user's custom workspace has minimized or compacted panel toolbars. It should complement, not replace, the full CaddyAI panel.
- `UX EXPECTATION`: The helper should clearly show which investigation it is scoped to and should update when the active investigation changes. If no investigation is active, it should fail closed into a choose-investigation prompt instead of guessing context.
- `RUNTIME EXPECTATION`: The helper should consume an explicit investigation id/scope token from the app state, not infer context from visible text or DOM scraping. It should request case data through existing typed hooks/tools and route/panel APIs.
- `RUNTIME EXPECTATION`: When asked to switch context, the helper should update investigation-backed panels to the selected investigation source without overwriting workspace layout templates or panel geometry.
- `SECDEVOPS GUARDRAILS`: Read actions can be passive by default, but write actions such as adding tasks, editing notes, changing IOC/task status, closing stale cases, or modifying investigation metadata must use the same CaddyAI write-tool approval/policy path already required elsewhere.
- `SECDEVOPS GUARDRAILS`: The helper must not silently cross investigation boundaries. Every read/write action should be scoped to the active investigation id and should surface the target investigation name/id before write confirmation.
- `SECDEVOPS GUARDRAILS`: The helper must not send email, run AgentCaddy actions, call local bridges, import/upload files, or execute external integrations merely because the mini helper was opened. First implementation smoke should be no-send/no-run.
- `TESTING EXPECTATION`: Add focused tests for no active investigation, active investigation scoping, investigation switch behavior, compact helper open/close/minimize, and write-action approval routing. Add browser smoke proving the button opens the helper from the investigation dropdown without changing workspace layout.
- `ACCESSIBILITY EXPECTATION`: The button needs a stable accessible name such as `Open investigation CaddyAI helper`, a visible active-scope label in the helper, keyboard access from the investigation dropdown, and screen-reader status when scope changes.
- `DEFERRED`: This planning item does not yet implement new CaddyAI tools, new write capabilities, autonomous stale-case closure, or a replacement for the full CaddyAI workspace. It should reuse existing CaddyAI capabilities first and only add tools after tool schema, executor, policy, and tests are aligned.

## Requirement Update - 2026-06-07 Snap/Dock Mosaic Behavior

Status: `ADDED TO ROLLOUT` for snap-zone visual semantics and workspace mosaic behavior. This is a planning/requirements update only; no source or standalone artifact changed in this note.

- `GOAL`: The workspace should feel like a moveable collage or mosaic of tools, not only a pile of independent floating windows.
- `FLOATING MODE`: Panels that are freely moved around the desktop can keep the current floating-window treatment: rounded shell, curved/glowing popout border language, header drag surface, and visible resize affordances.
- `SNAPPED MODE`: When a panel snaps to top, left, right, bottom, or a corner/zone, it should visually sit into the workspace as if docked. Snapped panels should use flatter/flush edges against the snap area, reduce or remove decorative external curved-window treatment on the snapped edges, and align to the workspace grid rather than appearing like an external overlay window.
- `MOSAIC LAYOUT`: Multiple snapped panels should seek available space and form a tiled/collage layout. Adjacent snapped panels should share resize seams where practical, so resizing one panel can adjust the neighboring cell instead of only resizing an isolated floating rectangle.
- `SMART-SNAP BORDER MERGE`: When workspace panels are placed flush against a side, corner, or neighboring panel, their borders should merge into a continuous joined block rather than showing separate rounded window tops/edges. If a small panel attaches to part of a longer side, the outside border should curve into that attached panel's border; if two panels attached to the same outer border meet, the external curve between them should drop and the panels should visually click together as one joined edge.
- `MERGED TOPOLOGY`: Border merging should work for one, two, three, or more connected panels. A partially filled grid should still accept a panel dropped into an interior gap; for example, an empty middle cell in a `3x3` layout should accept a ninth panel and then all surrounding shared borders should resolve into one connected mosaic block.
- `RESPONSIVE GRID DETECTION`: Add a workspace/screen-size reader that measures the current browser/workspace canvas and chooses available snap grids based on usable area. The system should not be limited to the current edge/corner placements. Larger workspaces should be able to expose layouts such as `3x2`, `4x2`, `4x4`, or `4x1` auto-placement lanes when there is enough room for usable panels.
- `AUTO PLACEMENT`: Drag/drop and popout placement should use the responsive grid model to suggest the best available cell or span. On small screens, fall back to simpler `1x1`, `1x2`, `2x1`, or `2x2` placements. On wide/large screens, allow more columns/rows and prevent panels from being assigned to occupied cells.
- `GRID SAFETY`: Minimum cell size should be derived from each panel's practical compact minimum, titlebar controls, and readable content. Do not expose a dense grid such as `4x4` if the resulting cells would clip controls, overlap text, or make the panel unusable.
- `RESIZE EXPECTATION`: Snapped/docked panels remain resizeable after snap. Resize handles should communicate shared-boundary resizing; the existing outside parallel-line hover effect remains appropriate for free-floating edges, but snapped shared seams may need a more docked/mosaic-specific seam highlight. Merged borders can keep faint border/highlight language on hover to show which side or seam remains draggable without visually separating the mosaic at rest.
- `IMPLEMENTATION NOTE`: Treat `floating`, `snapped`, and `docked` as visually distinct panel presentations even if they share geometry/state primitives. Future implementation should avoid hard-coding one floating chrome for every non-docked placement.
- `TEST NOTE`: Future Playwright coverage should verify a snapped panel has docked/mosaic chrome, flush alignment to the snap zone, no external curved line treatment on snapped edges, and working resize behavior after snap. Add multi-panel tests for left/right or top/bottom tiled layouts before promoting. Add viewport-size coverage proving small workspaces expose simple snap grids while larger workspaces expose safe `3x2`, `4x2`, `4x4`, or `4x1` options only when cells meet practical minimum dimensions. Add smart-snap tests for joined outer borders, two panels meeting on the same border, a small panel attached to a longer side, and an interior-hole drop such as filling the center of a `3x3` mosaic.

- `PARTIAL`: Manager agent Avicenna was queued for read-only selector review but did not return within the short wait. No agent feedback was counted as verified evidence for this slice.
- `OPEN`: Next likely slices remain layout template save/import/export with strict parser and panel allowlist, snapped mosaic chrome implementation, persisted snap geometry, CalendarCaddy event drawer compact behavior, and deeper drag/resize automation coverage.

## Requirement Update - 2026-06-07 CalendarCaddy Compact Chrome

Status: `ADDED TO ROLLOUT` for the next CalendarCaddy compact-layout slice. This note records user feedback only; the current implementation slice did not unwrap CalendarCaddy borders or move compact controls into the popout titlebar.

- `GOAL`: In compact/minimized CalendarCaddy popouts, reduce wasted vertical space above the calendar grid.
- `COMPACT HEADER`: When CalendarCaddy is compact, push the previous/today/month/year/view controls into the otherwise empty popout titlebar/header area where practical, instead of leaving a large blank band above the calendar.
- `ONE-LINE TARGET`: Prefer a single adaptive CalendarCaddy header/control row in compact popouts instead of separate popout-title and inner-calendar-control rows. If the panel is too narrow, minimize lower-value header elements before wrapping to a second line.
- `PRIORITY COLLAPSE ORDER`: In compact widths, hide or iconize the duplicate `CalendarCaddy` inner title first, then remove or iconize `Today`, then shorten view labels (`Week`/`Month`/`Year` to `W`/`M`/`Y` or icons) while preserving accessible names/tooltips. The current month/year selector and previous/next navigation remain higher priority than the duplicate label.
- `WORKWEEK OPTION`: Add a future setting for nonstandard week layouts. CalendarCaddy should eventually support a compact workweek mode such as Monday-Friday, plus configurable first day of week for odd schedules. This should affect calendar display semantics deliberately rather than being a hard-coded compact-only visual shortcut.
- `UNWRAP CALENDAR`: Reduce nested CalendarCaddy borders/wrappers inside the popout pane so the month grid uses more of the available panel footprint. Keep the ThreatCaddy border/glow language on the actual panel shell, but avoid redundant inner frames that consume usable space.
- `TEST NOTE`: Add compact Playwright coverage for header-control placement, one-line/no-wrap behavior at target widths, visible primary month navigation, accessible names for shortened labels, grid vertical footprint, configurable workweek/first-day behavior when implemented, and absence of redundant inner border wrappers before promotion.

## Requirement Update - 2026-06-07 Compact Panel Top-Bar Consolidation

Status: `ADDED TO ROLLOUT` for all compact/minimized workspace panels. This is a planning requirement only; no standalone artifact should be promoted from this note by itself.

- `SCOPE`: Applies to all compact, minimized, docked-mosaic, and floating workspace panels, not only CalendarCaddy or Notes.
- `TOP-BAR FIRST`: If secondary controls from a second toolbar row can fit in the popout titlebar/top bar, move or mirror them there in compact mode before keeping a separate second row.
- `COLLAPSE PRIORITY`: Consolidate common actions into the top bar first; then iconize or hide lower-value labels; preserve primary workflow actions; keep accessible names/tooltips for shortened controls.
- `WRAP RULE`: Do not wrap controls to a second line until the top bar cannot fit them without overlap, clipped text, or unusably small hit targets.
- `PANEL-SPECIFIC APPLICATIONS`: CalendarCaddy should prioritize month/year and previous/next controls; Notes and Tasks should prioritize new-item and sort/filter controls; investigation panels should prioritize case/context controls without serializing case data into layout templates.
- `TEST NOTE`: Add Playwright checks for no overlap, one-row top-bar behavior where width allows, accessible labels/tooltips, preserved primary actions, and collapsed/removed secondary toolbars before promotion.

## Requirement Update - 2026-06-08 Appearance Color Picker Eyedropper

Status: `ADDED TO ROLLOUT` for the Appearance/Odysseus theme repair lane. This is a planning/bug note only; no source or standalone artifact changed from this note.

- `BUG`: The visible eyedropper/dropper button in the color picker popover does not currently work in the standalone/in-app browser flow. The same failure has been observed with other color palette options above the shown picker.
- `SCOPE`: Audit all color-picker dropper buttons in Appearance settings, including ThreatCaddy palette fields, Odysseus theme palette options, Color Harmony accent controls, and background/effect color overrides.
- `EXPECTED`: A supported browser context should launch the native eyedropper, capture the selected color as a normalized sRGB hex value, update the active draft/input/swatches, and preserve the panel picker state without drifting to another palette field.
- `FALLBACK`: If `EyeDropper` or equivalent browser support is unavailable for the current standalone/browser context, the UI should not present a dead button. Disable or hide the dropper affordance and provide the existing manual hex/HSL/color-control path as the clear fallback.
- `TEST NOTE`: Add focused unit or component coverage for the unsupported-path behavior and a browser smoke where feasible for the supported-path call wiring. Also verify each palette/dropper instance targets its own color key and does not update unrelated accent/navigation fields.

## Requirement Update - 2026-06-08 Workspace-Affixed Panels And EmailCaddy Chrome

Status: `ADDED TO ROLLOUT` for the Phase 8 workspace panel overhaul. This is a planning/requirements update only; no source or standalone artifact changed from this note.

- `EMAILCADDY`: Remove or aggressively collapse the bulky EmailCaddy context/header block when EmailCaddy is popped out or affixed into the Workspace. The workspace panel titlebar/topbar should carry only the controls needed at that size, and the panel body should prioritize the mail list, selected message, split resize bar, and draft surface rather than duplicating the large route header shown in the normal route.
- `EMAILCADDY CONTEXT`: The old message-context popout pilot should not leave a large dead/source block in the EmailCaddy route or workspace when it has been popped out. If message context remains available, make it a compact docked/inline affordance or titlebar action rather than a full-width block that consumes the top of EmailCaddy.
- `EMAILCADDY DRAFT LIFECYCLE`: The email draft surface should only appear after the analyst chooses a reply action such as `Reply`, `Reply all`, or `Forward`; it should not occupy default EmailCaddy workspace real estate before that action. In the normal/full EmailCaddy frame, reply/draft UI should open inline inside the EmailCaddy panel without popout shadows or detached-window treatment. If EmailCaddy is itself popped out, affixed, or minimized in Workspace, reply can create a focused child draft popup/window that follows the same workspace panel rules and remains associated with EmailCaddy.
- `DRAFT CLOSE BEHAVIOR`: Draft windows/menus must be closable. If an unsent draft has unsaved changes, closeout should ask whether to save it as a draft before discarding or closing. The confirmation should preserve analyst intent and should not silently lose draft text, recipients, attachments, quoted context, or source attachment state.
- `EMAILCADDY CONTEXT TRIGGER`: Message context should not be persistently visible by default. It should appear only when AssistantCaddy is asked to work on the email, when the analyst presses a context/action button, or when a workflow explicitly needs quoted/source context. When invoked, context should follow the same presentation rule as reply/draft UI: inline inside a normal/full EmailCaddy frame, and as a compact child popup/window only when EmailCaddy is popped out, affixed, minimized, or too constrained for inline display.
- `WORKSPACE HEADER DEFAULT`: When any app panel is popped out, dragged into, snapped, or affixed inside Workspace, the workspace window header should become the primary panel header by default. Duplicate in-panel app headers/brand blocks such as `EmailCaddy`, `CalendarCaddy`, oversized unread/visible counters, large icon-title rows, and route-level chrome should disappear or collapse unless the panel is opened as a full route/full panel. Preserve accessible headings with `sr-only` or titlebar labels, and move essential actions into the compact workspace titlebar/topbar according to workflow priority.
- `NOTES/TASKS MINIMUM`: Notes and Tasks should continue shrinking below the current minimums wherever the titlebar buttons still fit. Minimum width/height should be governed by actual usable controls and content, not by older large defaults. Add tests that resize to the smallest button-safe size and verify no topbar overlap or hidden primary create/sort/view controls.
- `WORKSPACE HOME`: Objects dragged, popped out, or otherwise added into the Workspace should stay in the Workspace. Once a panel is workspace-owned, it should not visually or behaviorally float back into unrelated route space unless the user explicitly docks or closes it.
- `AFFIXED PANEL CHROME`: Future workspace-affixed panels should become seamless workspace tiles rather than external popout windows. When affixed/snapped into the workspace, panels should lose external-window corners, big shadows, and decorative floating treatment, while retaining resize handles, drag/arrange behavior, persistence, and compact titlebar controls.
- `MOSAIC VISUAL`: The long-term visual target is a custom page/collage builder: affixed panels line up as flat, connected sections with minimal seams, not a pile of overlapping rounded dialogs. Floating mode can keep popout styling; snapped/affixed mode should look integrated into the workspace surface.
- `GRID READER`: The seamless workspace tile system should use the same responsive workspace-size reader described in the snap/mosaic section, so affixed panel placement can graduate from basic edge/corner zones to safe `3x2`, `4x2`, `4x4`, or `4x1` grid options on larger screens.
- `TEST NOTE`: Add browser coverage that drags Notes/Tasks/EmailCaddy/CalendarCaddy into Workspace, verifies they remain workspace-owned across route navigation, verifies smallest practical dimensions, verifies no duplicate app header/brand block in workspace mode, verifies no large EmailCaddy route header/context block in compact workspace mode, verifies reply/draft/context surfaces appear only after the expected trigger, verifies unsaved draft close confirmation, and verifies affixed/snapped panels have flat/seamless chrome while remaining resizable.

## User QA Intake - 2026-06-08 15:55 EDT - Standalone Workspace/Panel Issues

Status: `INTAKE / ACTIVE FIXES STARTED`; EmailCaddy selected-message readout fix is now promoted.

- `DONE`: EmailCaddy selected-message readout is unwanted. Removed the lower `Selected email` header, subject/chips, and read-only body card from EmailCaddy while preserving explicit `Reply`, `Reply all`, `Forward`, `Context`, and draft actions.
- `DONE`: Minimized Notes panels now use a simplified selected-note editor in compact workspace mode. Remaining enhancement: compact title-line history navigation for older notes.
- `TODO`: Task list compact UX needs cleanup. Sub-items should persist expanded until toggled closed, compact task windows should scroll, hover text should expose full task titles, and compact rows may use two-line titles to preserve readability.
- `TODO`: Remove visible `Dock` nomenclature from compact/minimized sections. Use icons and hover text/tooltips for context where labels no longer fit.
- `TODO`: Calendar compact titlebar should progressively add/remove stamp icons between the move/resize/close controls as the calendar grows or shrinks so stamp affordances do not waste space or disappear abruptly.
- `TODO`: Snap guide behavior needs alignment with the visible purple/header guide line. Panels should actually snap to that guide rather than only showing it.
- `TODO`: Panel dragging should use all empty titlebar/header space, not only the directional cross handle. Controls remain no-drag zones.
- `DONE`: Workspace-owned popouts are now route-isolated from non-Workspace routes. Panels launched or dragged into Workspace stay mounted for Workspace restore but are hidden when the user selects Dashboard, CaddyAI, Timeline, Whiteboards, AssistantCaddy, FortuneINT, or other side-menu panels. See the 2026-06-08 Workspace Route Isolation promotion entry for evidence and residual test-cleanup notes.
- `TODO`: AssistantCaddy itself still needs draggable/poppable workspace panel behavior under the same ownership rules.
- `TODO`: Background effects need a follow-up visual parity pass against Odysseus. Sliders should have stronger visible effect; background hue and particle hue should support dual/color controls and default from theme/complementary/supplementary colors instead of fixed base colors.
- `TODO`: Left-side merged/snapped panels should shift horizontally with the sidebar/burger menu open-collapse cycle while maintaining current tile size and pulling attached panels with them.
- `TEST REQUIREMENT`: Each fix needs focused UI/browser coverage and standalone promotion only after source sanity, TypeScript, focused Vitest/Playwright, checkpoint, parity, and smoke pass.

## QA Intake - 2026-06-09 11:56 EDT - Workspace Layout, Dock Bars, Notes History, And Snap Policy

Status: `BACKLOGGED`. This is a ledger-only intake from user testing and does not modify source or standalone artifacts.

- `SNAPSHOT INVENTORY`: Before pruning, the canonical V3 snapshot store `/Users/brdavies/Documents/ThreatCaddy updates/ThreatCaddy-V3/.recovery-snapshots` contained `230` first-level recovery snapshot directories and was approximately `450 MB`. The broader `/Users/brdavies/Documents/ThreatCaddy updates` workspace had one `.recovery-snapshots` store.
- `DONE`: Pruned redundant recovery snapshots after a dry-run manifest. Deleted `206` intermediate snapshot directories and retained `24` strategic baselines. Final snapshot store size is approximately `42 MB`.
- `RETAINED BASELINES`: Kept the recovered EmailCaddy source-safety baseline, early workspace/provider/app-shell baselines, key global workspace milestones, selected Phase 8 smart-min/snap baselines, V3 consolidation, compact Notes/Tasks/chrome baselines, route-isolation and snap-boundary baselines, current 2026-06-09 AssistantCaddy/Calendar checkpoints, and the assistantcaddy handoff checkpoint.
- `SNAPSHOT POLICY`: Keep deliberate rollback baselines instead of snapshotting every planning note or visual-test iteration. Required snapshots remain: pre-risky source edit, pre-standalone promotion, post-standalone parity when useful, and hourly fallback/handoff checkpoints during long implementation runs. Ledger-only intake and screenshot-heavy QA notes should not create full recovery snapshots unless source risk is introduced. Prefer recording screenshot/video paths in the ledger and keeping bulky temporary QA captures in `/private/tmp` or the system temp folder.
- `BACKLOG`: Notes compact view looks materially improved, but it still needs a compact note-history selector/dropdown so analysts can switch to older notes without expanding the whole Notes route.
- `BACKLOG`: Minimized/docked workspace bars should feel built into the bottom workspace edge and lay side by side instead of becoming long full-width rows that do not stack well. Preserve the existing bottom button affordance, but make the container more integrated with the workspace surface.
- `BACKLOG`: Floating popups may expose partial offscreen edges while being dragged near workspace edges, but they must not be allowed to move above the point where their header cannot be reached again. Add clamp/recovery behavior for panels stuck at the top edge.
- `BACKLOG`: Workspace-owned snapped/merged items should sit behind active floating popouts. Among floating popouts, the last clicked panel should move to the top. Consider an optional setting where hovering a background popup briefly brings it forward for quick-glance review.
- `BACKLOG`: Task windows appear to have a maximum draggable height. They should be able to grow at least to the usable workspace height while preserving resize handles and scrollable content.
- `BACKLOG`: EmailCaddy and CalendarCaddy still need reliable drag-to-Workspace behavior from their panel/app surfaces, not just from sidebar launch paths.
- `BACKLOG`: Add an auto-snap sizing preference. Default candidate: snap a panel to the current shape/size it already has when possible, instead of always forcing equal-sized grid cells. Include a companion sort/even-layout action for users who prefer normalized tile sizes.
- `REGRESSION`: The snap/popout workspace behavior is leaking into normal ThreatCaddy side-menu panel clicks. All side-menu clicks should open their sections normally as route panels. Workspace panel behavior should only apply from the Workspace section, explicit pop-out controls, or explicit drag/drop into Workspace. Current behavior makes some normal side-menu panels act like the Workspace-owned version of that panel.
- `SECDEVOPS NOTE`: Treat these as separate geometry/chrome slices with focused tests. Do not combine notes history, dock-bar layout, z-order, top-edge clamp, task max-height, EmailCaddy/CalendarCaddy drag intake, route-panel click isolation, and snap-sizing preference into one source promotion unless the implementation naturally touches the same small module set.

## QA Intake - 2026-06-09 13:31 EDT / 2026-06-09 17:31 UTC - AI and Email Onboarding

Status: `BACKLOGGED`. This is a ledger-only intake from user direction and does not modify source or standalone artifacts.

- `BACKLOG`: Current ThreatCaddy AI and EmailCaddy onboarding is too weak. Build a guided onboarding flow that helps users choose AI platforms, assign models/providers to the right ThreatCaddy subsystem, and connect email providers without forcing manual trial-and-error.
- `BACKLOG`: AI setup should support user-discretion provider selection. The flow should ask what platform(s) the user wants to use and which systems should use them: for example CaddyAI with a cloud LLM such as ChatGPT/OpenAI, AssistantCaddy with a local model such as Ollama, or different providers per subsystem depending on workstation constraints, privacy needs, and available hardware.
- `BACKLOG`: AI onboarding should include prompts/scripts or guided setup cards for supported provider families. Candidate categories: local localhost LLM endpoints, OpenAI-compatible APIs, Ollama-style local models, and future custom provider adapters. Each card should explain what data leaves the machine, what credentials are needed, and what capability limits apply.
- `BACKLOG`: If local AI services already exist on the system, ThreatCaddy should be able to help detect them with explicit user consent. CaddyShack/localhost discovery can look for likely local endpoints and prefill candidate host/model settings, but the user must review and confirm before saving or sending any prompts.
- `BACKLOG`: Add provider health checks and model discovery where safe. The onboarding should verify reachability, list available models when the provider supports it, show context/window or capability hints when known, and fail closed without leaking secrets or prompt content into logs.
- `BACKLOG`: EmailCaddy onboarding should become provider-aware. Show recognizable provider choices with icons for common providers such as Google/Gmail, Proton, Outlook/Hotmail/Microsoft, and generic IMAP/POP3/SMTP. Provider capability details must be verified against current provider documentation during implementation because auth and app-password rules change over time.
- `BACKLOG`: For providers that support OAuth/SSO-style flows, EmailCaddy should open a clear login/authorization window and request narrowly scoped mail permissions. The UI should explain what access is requested, where tokens are stored, and how to revoke/disconnect.
- `BACKLOG`: Manual proxy/IMAP/POP3/SMTP setup should provide step-by-step provider-specific guidance when OAuth/SSO is unavailable or not chosen. Include how to find server names, ports, TLS/SSL settings, usernames, app passwords, and sending-server details. Include a safe connection-test step before enabling sync.
- `BACKLOG`: Email setup should distinguish read-only ingest, send/reply capability, draft-only mode, and external-send mode. Users should be able to start with a safer read-only or draft-only connection before granting send permissions.
- `SECDEVOPS NOTE`: Treat AI and email onboarding as a high-risk auth/credentials/local-bridge slice. Do not store raw passwords or API keys in logs, test fixtures, screenshots, ledger text, or localStorage without an explicit encrypted-storage design. Prefer OAuth tokens and OS/keychain-backed storage where available. Provider discovery must be opt-in, bounded to local/private endpoints unless the user explicitly authorizes external checks, and must never auto-send analyst case data during setup.
- `TESTING`: Future implementation needs focused tests for provider selection, no-secret logging, OAuth/manual setup branching, local endpoint discovery opt-in, failed connection handling, disconnect/revoke UI, and standalone/offline behavior. Browser smoke should verify icons, provider cards, manual setup guidance, and no unintended network calls before credentials are entered.
- `NEXT`: Slot this as a future onboarding slice after the current workspace geometry backlog unless the user prioritizes AI/email setup. Recommended first implementation cut: design provider-selection state and UI cards with no real credential storage, then add safe local-provider discovery and email manual setup guidance behind explicit user actions.

## QA Intake - 2026-06-09 15:29 EDT / 2026-06-09 19:29 UTC - Workspace Drag And Join-Wall Visual Cues

Status: `BACKLOGGED`. This is a ledger-only intake from user testing and does not modify source or standalone artifacts.

- `BACKLOG`: Workspace panels should be draggable into the workspace canvas without requiring the pointer to target one of the predefined workspace squares first. The operator should be able to place a panel freely, resize it, then choose whether to click/snap it into nearby workspace structure.
- `BACKLOG`: Snap/connect affordances should behave like a visual drag cue: when a moving panel approaches a joinable wall, edge, seam, or adjacent panel, the candidate joining wall should highlight. The panel should snap/connect on release, not during exploratory hover unless the user explicitly commits by dropping.
- `BACKLOG`: Task lists currently want to connect to the top workspace/context bar in some layouts. This may become acceptable after the second row is merged, but the snap rules should still prevent panels from joining above the intended workspace boundary or making headers unreachable.
- `BACKLOG`: Join cues should be consistent with the ThreatCaddy glow language: joining walls or seams should highlight with the existing border glow/adjacent-line vocabulary rather than square generic drop zones. The goal is a readable "these sides will join if released here" signal.
- `SECDEVOPS NOTE`: Treat this as a geometry/interaction slice. It should be implemented with browser drag tests that verify free placement, highlighted candidate join walls, release-to-snap behavior, workspace-boundary clamping, and no route-panel leakage.

## QA Intake - 2026-06-09 15:32 EDT / 2026-06-09 19:32 UTC - Workspace Header Consolidation And Layout Controls

Status: `BACKLOGGED`. This is a ledger-only intake from user testing and does not modify source or standalone artifacts.

- `BACKLOG`: Workspace currently has a header/selection behavior fight. When no investigation is selected, the investigation context bar disappears, but when an investigation is selected the workspace can show duplicate investigation/context rows. Consolidate this so selected investigation state is represented once.
- `BACKLOG`: In Workspace, prioritize top-row UI. Move the useful controls from the second row into the selected-investigation top row where space allows. The current lower row containing Workspace/CaddyShack/Investigations style buttons should go away when redundant in Workspace mode.
- `BACKLOG`: Make the top-row investigation name itself the investigation dropdown. Selecting a different investigation should update the active case context without duplicating a separate dropdown row.
- `BACKLOG`: Keep TLP/status visible in the consolidated header, but avoid turning the header into a two-line control stack unless the viewport truly requires it.
- `BACKLOG`: Workspace layout controls should drop the typed layout-name box from the main toolbar. Keep a `Layouts` dropdown with `Default` first, add an `Add layout` action inside the dropdown, and expose save/update/import/export as icon-first controls with hover text instead of visible words.
- `BACKLOG`: Add a compact icon-only update action for the active layout. The user-facing action can be a save/update icon with `title`/`aria-label`, not visible text.
- `BACKLOG`: The screenshot still shows the legacy large floating-source placeholder text `This pane is popped out in the workspace.` Confirm after the next standalone promotion that the active test URL is the current `4181` artifact. If the old block persists on a fresh `4181` reload, treat it as a regression in source-slot rendering or stale artifact serving.
- `SECDEVOPS NOTE`: Treat this as a header/layout-toolbar UX slice. It should not touch investigation data schemas or layout-template serialization until the visible consolidation is stable. Browser coverage should verify no duplicate investigation rows, top-row dropdown behavior, icon-only layout actions, and route/workspace isolation.

## Post-Test UX Repair Plan - 2026-06-09 20:16 EDT / 2026-06-10 00:16 UTC - Workspace Header, Dragging, Dock, and EmailCaddy Cleanup

Status: `PLANNED / DISPATCHED TO SLICES`. User testing on the promoted standalone found several workflow regressions and polish gaps. These are planned as bounded follow-up slices. No standalone promotion is authorized from slice threads; head chat remains responsible for final source reconciliation and promotion gates.

- `USER FEEDBACK`: Workspace currently shows two top bars. Merge them into one workspace/investigation bar. The top investigation name must become the primary dropdown, retaining TLP/status and layout controls without the duplicate second row.
- `USER FEEDBACK`: Notes is currently the best workspace panel behavior and should become the behavioral standard for other draggable panels. Notes still needs a clearer save/apply/back path after editing or creating a note so the user can return to the note selector/list.
- `USER FEEDBACK`: Other panels such as EmailCaddy, Tasks, CalendarCaddy, and the remaining Investigations panels still feel like they require a snap prompt to work. Dragged panels should be freely placeable like Notes, with optional snap/join cues rather than required snap selection.
- `USER FEEDBACK`: Remove the redundant bottom source/placeholder chip behavior for popped-out panels. Closing with `X` should remove the pane from the workspace entirely. Selecting the same side-menu item afterward should open the normal full panel route. Minimize may still send a panel to the bottom dock, but closed panels must not leave a dashed bottom placeholder or re-launch chip.
- `USER FEEDBACK`: EmailCaddy workspace popout still has a stale divider/line from the removed context section. Remove the leftover context/splitter chrome so resizing the bottom border resizes the actual mail surface rather than shrinking emails while preserving dead blank space.
- `TEAM PLAN`: Slice 1 owns the merged workspace/investigation header and top-row layout controls. Tests should cover selected/no-selected investigation states and absence of duplicate rows.
- `TEAM PLAN`: Slice 2 owns runtime drag/free-placement parity and snap-cue behavior so all investigation/assistant panels can launch into the workspace without a required snap prompt. Tests should compare Notes behavior against Tasks, CalendarCaddy, EmailCaddy, and at least two generic investigation panels.
- `TEAM PLAN`: Slice 3 owns close/minimize/source-placeholder semantics. `X` removes the workspace-owned panel; minimize uses the bottom dock only for intentionally minimized panels; source placeholders should not consume bottom workspace real estate. Tests should prove side-menu clicks still open the normal route after closing a workspace panel.
- `TEAM PLAN`: Slice 4 owns EmailCaddy workspace compact cleanup. Remove stale context divider remnants, fix bottom resize behavior, keep `Compose`, `Reply`, and `Context` accessible, and preserve expected EmailCaddy accessibility/test labels.
- `TEAM PLAN`: Slice 5 owns Notes polish plus cross-panel compact audit. Notes needs a save/apply/back-to-selector path. Broader panels should get compact topbar-first checks after Slice 2/3 semantics stabilize.
- `SECDEVOPS`: Each slice must create a pre-edit checkpoint, preserve `CadEmailWorkspace.tsx` line-count/export sanity, run focused TypeScript/Vitest/Playwright gates, and append concise ledger notes with write set, gates, blockers, and residual risks. Do not run `pnpm update:standalone` from slice threads.

## Head-Chat UX Standard - 2026-06-09 20:55 EDT / 2026-06-10 00:55 UTC - Dropdown Theme Unification

Status: `PLANNED FOR NEXT UI SOURCE PASS / NOT PROMOTED`. User testing confirms the Workspace investigation outline/dropdown and layout dropdown do not yet match the stronger ThreatCaddy dropdown visual language used by EmailCaddy.

- `USER FEEDBACK`: The investigation header outline and dropdown must align with ThreatCaddy theming. The EmailCaddy inbox/account/focus dropdowns are the visual template and should become the standard for all dropdown menus.
- `CANONICAL TEMPLATE`: `src/components/Common/ToolbarSelect.tsx` is the current reusable EmailCaddy dropdown primitive. It provides the rounded control shell, raised popup panel, selected-row active background, left glow bar, accent dot, hover state, and keyboard-accessible combobox/listbox behavior.
- `IMPLEMENTATION RULE`: Future dropdown work should prefer `ToolbarSelect` or a generalized variant of it over raw `<select>` controls when custom popup styling is required. Workspace investigation select, Workspace layout select, AssistantCaddy menus, settings dropdowns, and other app menus should converge on this component/pattern.
- `THEME RULE`: Selected menu rows should reuse burger/sidebar active language: active row fill, subtle glow, left-side glow/accent marker, and non-square rounded menu items. Native option styling alone is not sufficient for visible in-app dropdowns that need ThreatCaddy theming.
- `ACCESSIBILITY RULE`: The generalized dropdown must preserve labels, keyboard open/close, listbox/option semantics, outside-click close, Escape close, and deterministic test hooks. Where native select behavior is still required, keep global `select option` tokens as fallback only.
- `OWNER`: Next UI source pass should update Workspace investigation/layout dropdowns first, then audit remaining raw selects for conversion or explicit deferral. Add focused React/Playwright coverage that verifies selected investigation/layout dropdowns use the standard popup style and that options remain keyboard/click selectable.
- `PROMOTION RULE`: This is not a blocker for the already source-gated selected-investigation row fix, but it is a required polish item before declaring the Workspace header visually complete.

## Head-Chat UX Finding - 2026-06-09 21:25 EDT / 2026-06-10 01:25 UTC - EmailCaddy Bottom Slab, Context Strip, and Compact Search

Status: `ACTIVE BLOCKER FOR NEXT PROMOTION / ROUTED TO SLICE 4 / NOT PROMOTED`. User testing on the current `4181` standalone shows the EmailCaddy surface still reserves too much lower-pane space and exposes context/splitter chrome when it is not useful.

- `USER EVIDENCE`: The EmailCaddy lower area can show a large empty slab with a highlightable horizontal drag/splitter bar and a context strip. This consumes workspace height without showing mail reader, draft, or explicit context content.
- `USER EVIDENCE`: The current search control is too wide for compact mail use. Desired behavior is a short search field, roughly wide enough for the word `Search`, plus a small AI/Assistant button. Pressing Enter should perform normal search; pressing the AI button should route the query to the AssistantAI/CaddyAI helper flow.
- `USER EVIDENCE`: `Reply`, `Reply all`, and `Forward` controls should appear in the selected email reader/draft segment after an email is selected, including popped-out or compact workspace contexts. They should not live as a large standalone strip that leaves dead space.
- `REQUIRED BEHAVIOR`: Full and compact EmailCaddy must not allocate a lower pane, splitter, or context slab unless real selected-message, draft, draft-status, or explicit context content is visible. The `Resize selected email pane` separator should exist only when it resizes visible lower content.
- `REQUIRED BEHAVIOR`: The always-visible context bar/strip should be removed. Context remains available through the explicit `Context` action or assistant/requested context flow.
- `REQUIRED BEHAVIOR`: Preserve accessible contracts where applicable: `Compose`, `Reply`, `Reply all`, `Forward`, `Context`, `Resize selected email pane`, `Quoted context`, and `Source attachment available externally`.
- `TEAM STEER`: Slice 4 was sent the screenshot paths and required behavior. It must create a pre-edit checkpoint, verify `CadEmailWorkspace.tsx` line count/export sanity, run TypeScript/build-mode TypeScript as needed, focused EmailCaddy Vitest, focused Playwright for empty-state/no-dead-slab and selected-message controls, `git diff --check`, and ledger closeout.
- `PROMOTION RULE`: Do not promote standalone from head chat until this EmailCaddy source pass is source-gated or explicitly blocked with artifact evidence.

## Head-Chat Product Finding - 2026-06-09 21:38 EDT / 2026-06-10 01:38 UTC - Real AI, Email, and Slack Connector Onboarding

Status: `PRODUCT BACKLOG / CONNECTOR IMPLEMENTATION REQUIRED / BLOCKER BEFORE FUNCTIONAL CLAIMS`. User testing confirms the current AI and email onboarding cards are visibly design-only and do not prove that CaddyAI, AssistantCaddy, or EmailCaddy can connect to real provider accounts. This must be treated as a separate integration workstream, not as completed onboarding.

- `USER EVIDENCE`: Settings currently labels AI provider onboarding and email provider onboarding as `DESIGN ONLY`. The cards preview OpenAI-compatible, Ollama/local, generic AI, Gmail/Google, Proton, and Outlook/Microsoft paths, but they do not store credentials, start OAuth, probe local services, connect to mailboxes, or send/receive real data.
- `USER GOAL`: EmailCaddy needs a practical way to add and test real Gmail/Google, Proton, Outlook/Microsoft/Hotmail, and generic IMAP/SMTP accounts. The setup should feel like signing into the user's own email account, with provider icons, connection status, and an obvious test flow.
- `PLACEMENT RULE`: Email setup should not be buried inside the AI settings section. Primary entry points should exist in EmailCaddy and/or AssistantCaddy onboarding, with durable configuration mirrored under `Settings > Integrations` or a dedicated mail/integrations section. AI setup belongs in `Settings > AI`; mail setup belongs with EmailCaddy/integrations.
- `EMAIL IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENT`: Provider cards must graduate from design-only into real connector flows only after provider-specific auth and permission details are verified against current official documentation. Supported paths should include Google/Gmail OAuth where available, Microsoft/Outlook/Hotmail OAuth where available, Proton-specific bridge/provider constraints where applicable, and generic IMAP/SMTP manual setup with clear step-by-step guidance for host, port, TLS, username, app-password/proxy, and connection testing.
- `EMAIL SAFETY REQUIREMENT`: ThreatCaddy must not silently send mail. Connection tests should verify sign-in, read/list permissions, and optionally draft creation without external send. Sending must remain explicit and user-confirmed. Credentials/tokens must not be stored in plaintext localStorage; use an approved local secret/credential bridge or OS-backed storage design before real connector release.
- `AI IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENT`: CaddyAI should be treated as the currently functional AI baseline and must not be regressed by onboarding changes. The gap is AssistantCaddy/provider onboarding clarity: users should be able to choose which LLM backs AssistantCaddy and any related helper flows, such as OpenAI-compatible API, local Ollama/localhost model, CaddyAI-backed routing, or another verified adapter. Local discovery/probing, including any CaddyShack localhost helper, must be explicit and user-initiated, with health-check status and no background probing.
- `AI UX REQUIREMENT`: The top AI provider cards should become a guided setup wizard or compact provider picker with actual connection test results. The UX should clearly distinguish configured, connected, failed, and design-placeholder states.
- `SLACK IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENT`: Add a Slack integration workstream for notifications and message triage. Desired behavior includes notifications for direct mentions, one-to-one DMs, group DMs, and channel/thread replies after the user posts, with controls to avoid noisy team/channel floods. Group DMs and formal channel/team spaces should be distinguishable in the UI.
- `SLACK SAFETY REQUIREMENT`: Slack connection must use explicit workspace/user authorization, scoped permissions, and a clear notification policy. It must not scrape or persist Slack content beyond the user's configured scope. Notification matching should be explainable, including whether it is a direct mention, DM, group DM, thread reply, or channel follow-up.
- `TEST PLAN`: Add mocked connector tests for provider selection, failed auth, successful auth, token revocation, connection-test status, and no-send-by-default behavior. Add manual smoke checklists for real test accounts only after credentials and provider docs are verified. Add Playwright coverage for EmailCaddy/AssistantCaddy onboarding entry points, provider icons, configuration status, and Slack notification policy controls.
- `PROMOTION RULE`: The current UI may remain design-only during the workspace overhaul, but the product must not claim functional EmailCaddy mail integration, AssistantCaddy provider onboarding, or Slack integration until this connector workstream is source-gated with tests and documented limitations. Preserve existing CaddyAI functionality while clarifying what is configured, connected, mocked, or still design-only.

Selecting or right-clicking a message should open a dedicated reader popout/inline modal rather than permanently allocating the lower selected-message body section.

## Slice 2 Follow-up Source Gate - 2026-06-10 08:46 EDT / 2026-06-10 12:46 UTC - EmailCaddy and CalendarCaddy Snap Parity

Status: `SOURCE-GATED / NOT PROMOTED`. This follow-up repairs the EmailCaddy/CalendarCaddy border-snap divergence reported from standalone runtime testing. `pnpm update:standalone` was not run.

- `CHECKPOINT`: Created `.recovery-snapshots/2026-06-09-slice-2-email-calendar-snap-parity-pre-edit/` from `node scripts/assistantcaddy-rollout-checkpoint.mjs --id 2026-06-09-slice-2-email-calendar-snap-parity-pre-edit`. - `ROOT CAUSE`: EmailCaddy's larger minimum panel size can exceed a one-cell right-edge or corner snap at common desktop canvas widths. The prior snap selector then skipped the expected edge/corner candidate and fell back to weaker placement behavior, which looked like a floating/window-border selection path. CalendarCaddy has a smaller minimum size, so the same bug was less obvious but still shared the geometry path.
- `DONE`: `selectWorkspaceGridPlacementForPointer` now preserves the existing one-cell snap candidates first, then selects the smallest valid edge/corner-anchored multi-cell span when the dragged panel minimum size cannot fit a single edge cell. The selected placement retains the legacy snap-zone label (`right`, `top-left`, and related edge/corner zones) so previews, join-wall cues, and mosaic metadata remain compatible.
- `DONE`: Occupied-cell and minimum-size rules remain enforced for adaptive spans. Large EmailCaddy panels can snap to right borders and corners without requiring a placement prompt, while Notes/Tasks continue to keep their previous compact one-cell snap behavior where it fits.
- `DONE`: Focused Playwright now compares Notes, Tasks, EmailCaddy, and CalendarCaddy through free launch, border/corner snap, optional visual snap cue before release, and snapped/affixed state after release. The browser test asserts only the dragged panel dialog exists during snap drag, guarding against a required placement prompt or extra window-border selection overlay.
