## Slice 2 Follow-up Source Gate - 2026-06-10 14:15 EDT / 2026-06-10 18:15 UTC - Workspace Click-Stick Placement and Second-Panel Seam Priority

Status: `SOURCE-GATED / NOT PROMOTED`. This follow-up addresses `Head-Chat Runtime Finding - 2026-06-10 - Workspace Click-Stick Placement and Second-Panel Oversized Region Bug`. The fix is geometry/UI-only. `pnpm update:standalone` was not run.

- `CHECKPOINT`: Created `.recovery-snapshots/2026-06-10-slice-2-click-stick-second-panel-pre-edit/` from `node scripts/assistantcaddy-rollout-checkpoint.mjs --id 2026-06-10-slice-2-click-stick-second-panel-pre-edit`. Checkpoint reported `HTML parity: pass` and sidecar parity pass for `browser-ponyfill-C8fpMoVO.js`, `chunk-reload-guard.js`, and `search.worker-CbO64xRP.js`.
- `WRITE SET`: `src/components/WorkspacePanels/workspaceGrid.ts`, `src/components/WorkspacePanels/WorkspacePanel.tsx`, `src/__tests__/workspace-grid.test.ts`, `e2e/workspace-launch-free-placement.spec.ts`, this ledger, and `docs/assistantcaddy-workspace-overhaul-handoff-2026-06-06.md`.
- `ROOT CAUSE`: `selectWorkspaceGridPlacementForPointer` entered the broad active edge/corner branch before local attached-placement scoring. When a second panel was dragged in the top activation band near an existing panel side, a broad top/top-half candidate could win even though the pointer was closer to an exposed neighbor seam.
- `DONE`: Added a nearest seam/border scoring pass ahead of broad grid allocation. The pass evaluates local workspace borders, exposed neighbor seams, and interior holes by pointer-to-seam distance before considering broad edge/corner grids or adaptive anchored spans.
- `DONE`: Local candidates now include minimum-fitting spans, not only one-cell placements. This lets larger panels such as EmailCaddy attach beside an existing tile when a single local seam cell is too small, while still avoiding unrelated top-half previews.
- `DONE`: WorkspacePanel drag now passes the moving panel's current geometry as preferred size hints. The scorer uses those hints to choose the local cell/span closest to the panel's current footprint, so snapping preserves size better and auto-equalized grid sizing is not the default.
- `DONE`: Visual preview and join-cue behavior remains tied to the selected local placement. Browser coverage asserts the preview starts on the exposed seam, the preview height stays below broad top-half allocation, the neighbor seam cue is visible, and the final snapped panels share a contiguous seam.
- `SOURCE SANITY`: Final current-source sanity: `CadEmailWorkspace.tsx` `2075` lines with `export const CadEmailWorkspace = EmailCaddyWorkspace;` at line `2075`; `workspaceGrid.ts` `1372`; `WorkspacePanel.tsx` `1564`; `workspace-grid.test.ts` `514`; `workspace-launch-free-placement.spec.ts` `300`.
- `GATES`: TypeScript passed: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.
- `GATES`: Build-mode TypeScript passed: `pnpm exec tsc -b --pretty false`.
- `GATES`: Focused Vitest passed: `pnpm exec vitest run src/__tests__/workspace-grid.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=60000 --pool=threads --no-file-parallelism` (`18` tests). The known `--localstorage-file` warning remained non-fatal.
- `GATES`: Focused Playwright passed: `pnpm exec playwright test e2e/workspace-launch-free-placement.spec.ts --project=chromium --workers=1 --reporter=line --retries=0` (`23` Chromium tests). New second-panel cases cover `Notes -> EmailCaddy`, `Notes -> Tasks`, `Tasks -> Evidence`, `EmailCaddy -> CalendarCaddy`, and `Tasks -> Timeline`.
- `GATES`: `git diff --check` passed after the source/test/docs updates. Provenance caveat remains: this checkout reports repository contents as untracked, so `git diff --check` is a whitespace/syntax hygiene gate rather than reliable changed-file provenance.
- `SAFETY`: No data schemas, Dexie storage, investigation/task/note/email/calendar persistence, connector credentials, provider APIs, CaddyAI/agent behavior, external network calls, or standalone artifacts were modified.
- `BLOCKERS`: No source blockers remain for the click-stick and second-panel seam-priority fix.
- `HANDOFF`: Later promotion must be coordinated from head chat with pre-standalone checkpoint, source-gate confirmation, artifact refresh, parity/hashes, and `4181` smoke unless directed otherwise.
- `RESIDUAL`: The shared geometry path now covers current workspace-capable panels, but future panels with minimum sizes larger than any available local span will still fall back to broad/adaptive placement because no valid local seam placement exists. Existing Vite dynamic/static import and chunk-size warnings remain non-fatal.

## Head-Chat Product Finding - 2026-06-10 - Integrations Settings Source-Type Redesign

Status: `BACKLOG / LEDGER ONLY`. This captures the next Integrations settings direction and provider catalog expansion. No source UI, connector, credential, schema, network, or standalone artifact changes were made for this entry.

- `USER REQUIREMENT`: `Settings > Integrations` should become a large responsive source-type dashboard. Wide layouts should use `2-3` columns; narrower layouts should stack groups vertically. Integration groups should be collapsible/minimizable because the catalog will become long.
- `UI GROUPING REQUIREMENT`: Organize integrations by source/function instead of one flat list. Initial top-level groups should include `Email`, `Messaging`, `Threat Intelligence`, `Malware Analysis / Sandbox`, and `SIEM / SOAR`. Email and messaging may stay as separate first-class groups rather than being buried under generic integrations.
- `DROPDOWN / MENU REQUIREMENT`: Provider pickers, group filters, and action menus in this area should reuse the EmailCaddy dropdown/menu visual language: themed outline, soft glow/active highlight, compact icon affordances, and hover text for dense controls. Avoid default square browser-like dropdown styling.
- `STARTER CATALOG - EMAIL`: Gmail / Google Workspace, Outlook / Microsoft Graph, Proton Mail / Bridge-assisted local mail client path, and generic IMAP/SMTP. Email setup must remain consent-driven and should guide users from EmailCaddy and/or AssistantCaddy into Integrations instead of hiding mail setup inside AI settings.
- `STARTER CATALOG - MESSAGING`: Slack, Microsoft Teams, Discord, Mattermost, and generic webhook. Slack should preserve the earlier requirement for direct mentions, one-to-one DMs, group DMs, and thread/channel follow-up notifications with noise controls.
- `STARTER CATALOG - THREAT INTELLIGENCE`: Team Cymru / Pure Signal / Scout, MISP, OpenCTI, VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB, GreyNoise, Shodan, Censys, AlienVault OTX, Recorded Future, Flashpoint, Anomali ThreatStream, ThreatConnect, ThreatQuotient, Cyware, urlscan.io, URLhaus / MalwareBazaar, and generic TAXII/STIX feed. Cymru is explicitly required; it should not be omitted from the first serious integration catalog pass.
- `STARTER CATALOG - MALWARE ANALYSIS / SANDBOX`: VirusTotal, Hybrid Analysis, ANY.RUN, Joe Sandbox, Intezer, CAPE/Cuckoo, MalwareBazaar, URLhaus, and generic file/hash detonation or enrichment adapter. This group should distinguish passive hash/URL enrichment from active sample submission/detonation because the latter has stronger privacy and handling implications.
- `STARTER CATALOG - SIEM / SOAR`: Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk Enterprise/Splunk SOAR, Elastic Security, Google SecOps / Chronicle, IBM QRadar, Palo Alto Cortex XSOAR, TheHive/Cortex, Shuffle, Tines, Torq, ServiceNow SecOps, and generic SIEM query/webhook adapter.
- `CARD MODEL`: Each provider card should expose type, status (`Not configured`, `Configured`, `Connected`, `Failed`, `Revoked`, `Design only`), supported entity types, auth method, last connection test, and whether it can read, write, submit samples, trigger workflows, or notify. Cards should be compact by default with an expandable details/setup view.
- `SEARCH / FILTER REQUIREMENT`: Add search and filters across provider name, group, supported entity type (`IP`, `domain`, `URL`, `hash`, `email`, `case`, `message`, `alert`), auth method, write capability, and offline/local capability.
- `SECDEVOPS REQUIREMENT`: All real connectors need explicit user consent, least-privilege scopes, per-provider connection tests, clear read/write/sample-submission warnings, token revocation UX, no-send/no-submit defaults, and no background probing unless the user triggers discovery or enables a scheduled policy. Secrets must not be written to logs, exported layouts, screenshots, or local handoff files.
- `IMPLEMENTATION RULE`: Treat this entry as a product/design backlog item until implementation. Before building real connectors, re-check current provider docs and terms for OAuth/API scope, rate limits, data-retention implications, and allowed automation. Mock connector tests should precede real credential flows.
- `TEST PLAN`: Add responsive UI tests for `3-column`, `2-column`, and stacked layouts; group collapse/expand persistence; provider-card status transitions; search/filter behavior; blocked credential export; no network calls before explicit connect/test; and provider-specific mocked success/failure/revocation flows.
- `RESEARCH REFERENCES`: Current planning was grounded against public provider docs/pages: VirusTotal API v3 overview (`https://docs.virustotal.com/reference/overview`), Microsoft Sentinel threat intelligence connector guidance (`https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/connect-threat-intelligence-tip`), Slack APIs (`https://docs.slack.dev/apis/`), AbuseIPDB APIv2 docs (`https://docs.abuseipdb.com/`), MISP project overview (`https://www.misp-project.org/`), OpenCTI connector docs (`https://docs.opencti.io/latest/deployment/connectors/`), Team Cymru public product/integration page (`https://www.team-cymru.com/`), Gmail API overview (`https://developers.google.com/workspace/gmail/api/guides`), and Proton Bridge support (`https://proton.me/support/bridge`).

## Head-Chat Runtime Finding - 2026-06-10 - Workspace Click-Stick Placement and Second-Panel Oversized Region Bug

Status: `BACKLOG BUG / LEDGER ONLY`. User runtime testing on the promoted standalone at `4181` showed the first panel can be added to Workspace acceptably, but adding a second panel can create an oversized top-half snap/selection region and consume that space instead of attaching locally. No source, test, artifact, or standalone changes were made for this entry.

- `USER EVIDENCE`: Screenshot `2026-06-10 12:57 EDT` shows a Notes panel placed against the left/top workspace area and an EmailCaddy roll-up/floating control in the middle. The active workspace preview has a large highlighted top-half rectangle that should not appear for a local second-panel placement.
- `EXPECTED BEHAVIOR`: Workspace panels should support a default click/stick or drop/stick behavior. If a panel is dropped adjacent to any workspace border or another panel seam, it should attach to that nearest valid border/seam by default.
- `PANEL COVERAGE`: The same placement semantics should apply across core and investigation panels: border/workspace shell, Notes, Tasks, Evidence, Products, Timeline, Whiteboards, IOCs, Graph, Activity, EmailCaddy, CalendarCaddy, and other future workspace-capable side-menu panels.
- `PLACEMENT PRIORITY`: Nearest seam/border attachment should win before broad grid allocation. The placement engine should prefer local workspace-edge or neighbor-seam attachment when the pointer is near an edge, panel side, or panel corner. Larger adaptive spans should be a fallback only when the panel cannot fit the local seam/cell or no valid local placement exists.
- `BUG DESCRIPTION`: The second-panel flow appears to let adaptive top/large-region selection outrank the local border/seam intent. This creates a large top-half reserved region and makes the next panel feel like it is snapping to a coarse predefined grid instead of sticking to the operator's target border/panel.
- `UX REQUIREMENT`: Visual cues should show the actual snap target: highlighted joining wall, near-edge glow, seam/corner cue, and no oversized unrelated preview. If the panel will attach to the left side of an existing note, the cue should be on that side; if it will attach to the workspace border, the cue should be on that border.
- `SIZE REQUIREMENT`: When a panel is already sized, snap should preserve its current dimensions as much as possible and attach it to the selected seam/border. Auto-equalized grid sizing should be optional, not the default behavior.
- `IMPLEMENTATION HINT`: Inspect `selectWorkspaceGridPlacementForPointer`, adaptive anchored placement, occupant collision, and snap-preview rendering. Add a nearest-candidate scoring pass that ranks local seams/borders by pointer distance before considering large `top`, `bottom`, or multi-cell adaptive spans.
- `TEST PLAN`: Add regression coverage that opens one panel, snaps/sticks it to a border, then adds a second panel near each exposed side/corner and verifies no unrelated top-half preview appears. Cover at least Notes -> EmailCaddy, Notes -> Tasks, Tasks -> Evidence, EmailCaddy -> CalendarCaddy, and one non-Assistant investigation panel. Assertions should verify `data-workspace-panel-chrome="snapped"`, the expected attached side attributes, no oversized `data-workspace-snap-preview` for unrelated regions, and contiguous seams after release.
- `SAFETY`: This should be treated as workspace geometry/UI behavior only. It should not touch investigation data, notes/tasks/email/calendar content, connectors, credentials, import/export, or standalone promotion until source gates pass.

## Slice 2 Source Gate - 2026-06-10 17:07 EDT / 2026-06-10 21:07 UTC - Horizontal Neighbor Seam Repair

Status: `SOURCE-GATED / NOT PROMOTED`. The top/bottom neighbor-seam gap from the head-chat audit is repaired in source. `pnpm update:standalone` was not run.

- `CHECKPOINT`: Created `.recovery-snapshots/2026-06-10-slice-2-horizontal-seam-pre-edit/` with `node scripts/assistantcaddy-rollout-checkpoint.mjs --id 2026-06-10-slice-2-horizontal-seam-pre-edit`; checkpoint reported standalone HTML parity pass and sidecar parity pass.
- `WRITE SET`: `src/components/WorkspacePanels/workspaceGrid.ts`, `src/__tests__/workspace-grid.test.ts`, `e2e/workspace-panel-snapped-layout.spec.ts`, this ledger, and `docs/assistantcaddy-workspace-overhaul-handoff-2026-06-06.md`.
- `ISSUE`: Horizontal seam placement returned no `neighbor-seam` cue for a Tasks panel dropped below a snapped top-left Notes tile because the selection path could exit before scoring interior neighbor seams, and same-grid direct seam coordinates could be rounded into the occupied neighbor cell.
- `FIX`: `selectWorkspaceGridPlacementForPointer` now evaluates nearest seam candidates before the no-active-edge return. Direct neighbor seam candidates preserve panel dimensions where practical, use anchored metadata for same-grid snapped neighbors to avoid occupied-cell rejection, and use fine-grid exact seam placement for other neighbor fits. Side attachments clamp along a longer neighbor side when they fit.
- `FIX`: Mosaic corner classification now uses a small corner coverage tolerance while preserving strict seam attach tolerance, so longer-side attachments keep integrated outer curves without loosening actual snap adjacency.
- `TEST UPDATE`: Added a Vitest regression for selecting a below-panel horizontal seam. Updated the vertical seam Playwright assertion to measure available min-size transfer headroom instead of assuming an 80px resize transfer after size-preserving snap placement.
- `SOURCE SANITY`: `src/components/CaddyAssistant/CadEmailWorkspace.tsx` remained `2075` lines with `export const CadEmailWorkspace = EmailCaddyWorkspace;` at line `2075`.
- `GATES`: Passed `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.
- `GATES`: Passed focused Vitest: `pnpm exec vitest run src/__tests__/workspace-grid.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=60000 --pool=threads --no-file-parallelism` (`19` passed). The known `--localstorage-file` warning remained non-fatal.
- `GATES`: Passed focused horizontal seam Playwright grep for `resizes top and bottom snapped tiles through their shared horizontal seam` (`1` Chromium test).
- `GATES`: Passed targeted vertical seam and longer-side outer-curve Playwright greps after assertion/corner-tolerance repairs (`1` Chromium test each).
- `GATES`: Passed full focused Chromium suite: `pnpm exec playwright test e2e/workspace-launch-free-placement.spec.ts e2e/workspace-panel-snapped-layout.spec.ts e2e/workspace-panels-smoke.spec.ts --project=chromium --workers=1 --reporter=line` (`75` passed).
- `GATES`: `git diff --check` passed after the source/test/docs updates. A direct trailing-whitespace scan across touched files also passed because the checkout is all-untracked and plain git diff provenance is limited.

## Head-Chat Hotfix - 2026-06-10 18:50 EDT / 2026-06-10 22:50 UTC - Compact EmailCaddy Draft Popout

## TC V3 Onboarding Slice 1 - 2026-06-11 20:33 EDT / 2026-06-12 00:33 UTC - Email Connector Architecture

Status: `SOURCE-GATED / NOT PROMOTED`. This slice adds a safe local EmailCaddy account onboarding architecture without real OAuth, provider calls, IMAP/SMTP sockets, send behavior, or plaintext credential storage. `pnpm update:standalone` was not run.

- `CHECKPOINT`: Created `.recovery-snapshots/2026-06-10-slice-email-architecture-pre-edit/` with `node scripts/assistantcaddy-rollout-checkpoint.mjs --id 2026-06-10-slice-email-architecture-pre-edit`; checkpoint reported HTML parity pass and sidecar parity pass.
- `WRITE SET`: `src/lib/email-onboarding.ts`, `src/hooks/useEmailAccounts.ts`, `src/hooks/useSettings.ts`, `src/types.ts`, `src/__tests__/email-onboarding.test.ts`, this ledger, and `docs/assistantcaddy-workspace-overhaul-handoff-2026-06-06.md`.
- `INSPECTED`: `src/db.ts`, `src/types.ts`, `src/hooks/useSettings.ts`, `src/hooks/useIntegrations.ts`, `src/lib/backup-data.ts`, `src/lib/backup-restore.ts`, `src/lib/backup-crypto.ts`, `src/lib/encryptionStore.ts`, `src/lib/encryptionMiddleware.ts`, `src/lib/export.ts`, `src/types/integration-types.ts`, `src/lib/integration-executor.ts`, and current EmailCaddy/Settings design-only references.
- `DECISION`: No new Dexie table was added in this slice. Existing Settings persistence is plaintext JSON in `localStorage`; Dexie field encryption exists but is session-key dependent and does not provide an approved email secret store. This slice stores only sanitized account metadata and external/local-bridge credential references.
- `DONE`: Added `src/lib/email-onboarding.ts` with provider metadata for Google/Gmail, Microsoft Outlook/Hotmail, Proton Bridge, Generic IMAP/SMTP, and Manual local bridge/proxy. Provider capabilities are explicit and `sendEnabledByDefault` is always `false`.
- `DONE`: Added safe account status model: `not_configured`, `pending`, `connected`, `failed`, `revoked`, and `design_only/mock_only`. Added transition helpers for pending/connected/failed/revoked.
- `DONE`: Added sanitizer allowlists for account metadata and credential references. Raw fields such as passwords, OAuth access/refresh tokens, app passwords, API keys, and nested token values are not persisted.
- `DONE`: Added safe mock/local connection-test plumbing. It never calls providers, OAuth endpoints, IMAP/SMTP sockets, or `fetch`. Without an approved credential reference/local bridge it fails closed with `needs_provider_oauth`, `needs_local_bridge`, or `local_bridge_unavailable`. Explicit mock success is only available through `allowMockConnected`.
- `DONE`: Added `useEmailAccounts` as a future UI-facing hook over existing settings storage. It persists sanitized metadata only and supports add/update/revoke/mark-pending/mock-test flows.
- `DONE`: `useSettings` now sanitizes `emailAccounts` during load/migration and before persistence.
- `COVERAGE`: `src/__tests__/email-onboarding.test.ts` covers provider metadata, no-send defaults, sanitizer/type validation, secret stripping, status transitions, failed OAuth/local-bridge tests, explicit mock success, revoked state, settings persistence boundaries, and JSON export excluding email account metadata/secrets.
- `SOURCE SANITY`: `src/components/CaddyAssistant/CadEmailWorkspace.tsx` is `2629` lines and ends with `export const CadEmailWorkspace = EmailCaddyWorkspace;`.
- `GATES`: Passed `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.
- `GATES`: Focused Vitest passed: `pnpm exec vitest run src/__tests__/email-onboarding.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=60000 --pool=threads --no-file-parallelism` (`10` passed). The known `--localstorage-file` warning remained non-fatal.
- `GATES`: Broadened focused Vitest passed: `pnpm exec vitest run src/__tests__/email-onboarding.test.ts src/__tests__/useSettings.test.ts src/__tests__/export.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=60000 --pool=threads --no-file-parallelism` (`76` passed). The known `--localstorage-file` warning remained non-fatal.
- `SAFETY`: No OAuth implementation, provider network call, IMAP/SMTP socket, local bridge fetch, credential value, send path, SettingsPanel UI, EmailCaddy UI, Dexie schema migration, backup/restore table set, integration executor behavior, or standalone artifact was changed.
- `BLOCKERS`: No source blocker remains for the bounded architecture contract. Real provider onboarding remains blocked on an approved OAuth/local bridge/secret-store design and provider-specific current docs.
- `RESIDUAL`: Existing `Settings` still contains older plaintext LLM/API settings outside this slice; this work does not remediate those. Email account metadata in `localStorage` can include non-secret labels/addresses and should be treated as local metadata. Future slices must add consent UI, provider docs verification, local bridge capability discovery, encrypted/external secret storage, and explicit send approval before claiming real connection or send support.

### Hotwash - TC V3 Onboarding Slice 1 Email Connector Architecture

- `WHAT WORKED`: Keeping the slice as a pure contract/model layer avoided premature OAuth, IMAP/SMTP, provider-network, UI, and Dexie migration risk while still creating a reusable provider/status/capability shape for future EmailCaddy onboarding.
- `WHAT WORKED`: Sanitizing through both the onboarding helpers and `useSettings` gave the tests a concrete no-plaintext-secret boundary instead of relying on caller discipline.
- `WHAT SLOWED DOWN`: Existing settings persistence is plaintext `localStorage`, while field encryption exists elsewhere with session-key assumptions. That required explicit verification and a narrower metadata-only design.
- `WHAT SLOWED DOWN`: The checkout reports the repository as all-untracked, so changed-file provenance and `git diff --check` remain weaker than normal tracked-diff review.
- `VERIFY BEFORE PROMOTION`: Rerun source sanity, TypeScript, focused email-onboarding/settings/export Vitest, `git diff --check`, and a pre-standalone checkpoint; verify no SettingsPanel/EmailCaddy UI now claims real provider login, sync, or send support from this model-only slice.
- `VERIFY BEFORE PROMOTION`: Confirm exports/backups still do not serialize email account metadata or secret-like fields, and confirm `sendEnabledByDefault` remains false for every email provider.
- `MEMORY-CANDIDATE`: For connector architecture slices, first prove storage boundaries and failure states with tests before adding UI claims or network behavior.
- `MEMORY-CANDIDATE`: In multi-chat rollout work, each source-gated slice should close with an explicit product hotwash plus separate `MEMORY-CANDIDATE` process lessons, without writing global memory from the worker chat.
- `DO NOT REPEAT`: Do not treat design-only onboarding UI as functional integration evidence.
- `DO NOT REPEAT`: Do not add credential fields to `Settings` or exports as a shortcut around missing secret-store/local-bridge architecture.
- `DO NOT REPEAT`: Do not promote standalone from a source-only connector foundation until head-chat reruns the coordinated promotion gate.

### Hotwash - Slice 3 EmailCaddy Account Flow

- `CONTRACT STATUS`: Slice 1 provider metadata was available and reused. A temporary UI-local setup state contract was created for this slice; head-chat should reconcile it with the future approved secret-store/connector implementation.

## User Feedback Intake - Email/Integration Onboarding Direction - 2026-06-12 12:31 EDT / 2026-06-12 16:31 UTC

Status: `PRODUCT FEEDBACK / NOT SOURCE-GATED`. User clarified several contention points during the active manifest-only loop. This is product direction and should stay in ledger/handoff, not process memory.

- `EMAIL ONBOARDING DIRECTION`: The user does not want email onboarding to remain a simple placeholder or a plain "type your info" form buried in email settings. Future live onboarding should behave more like a real OAuth/SSO or username/password sign-in flow where appropriate, with explicit auth/consent boundaries and safe credential handling.
- `AI SETTINGS BOUNDARY`: The earlier issue where email provider onboarding appeared in the AI section remains important. AI setup should not own email provider login. Email/account onboarding should live in the correct Integrations/EmailCaddy surface, with AI setup only linking out when needed.
- `INTEGRATIONS DIRECTION`: The user wants more integrations attached, organized, and sorted. Prior catalog work added grouped local metadata and a passive Integrations dashboard, but real activation, sorting polish, and connector-specific onboarding remain product work.
- `SLACK/DM NOTIFICATIONS`: Prior source work modeled Slack notification policy defaults for direct mentions, one-to-one DMs, group DMs, thread replies, channel follow-ups, and noise controls without connecting to Slack or storing content. Editable Slack policy controls and real Slack API/DM notification behavior remain future gated work.
- `CURRENT LOOP CAVEAT`: The active operations/live implementation manifest loop is still manifest/plan-only. It should not be misreported as implementing real OAuth, Slack APIs, credential storage, provider sync/send, or live notification delivery unless a later head-chat source gate explicitly accepts that broader work.

Slice 4 accepted a callable injected test-double transport whose function body could perform side effects before post-result checks, and lacked root exact-key validation. Slice 5 could let webhook URL-shaped handles pass through the store boundary via the shared credential reference validator and had generic rather than explicit live-action/webhook/send field coverage.

- `REPAIR SUMMARY`: Messaging now exact-key-validates root input and accepts reviewed no-call adapter contracts without emitting a downstream-incompatible `executableContract` decision key. Local bridge validates nested execution metadata shape before every read. LLM exact-key-validates root input and validates injected transport metadata while keeping execution disabled. Credential store rejects URL-shaped handle IDs before treating them as opaque handles and explicitly classifies live-action/webhook/send fields. Tests now cover allowed-field object/function poisoning where applicable.
- `FOCUSED RERUNS`: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. `pnpm exec tsc -b --pretty false` passed. Repaired exact Vitest bundle passed `5` files / `44` tests. Worker-focused Vitest bundles passed: provider `4` files / `29` tests, messaging `4` / `27`, local bridge `4` / `27`, LLM `4` / `35`, credential `4` / `36`. Known non-fatal `--localstorage-file` warnings remained.
- `STATIC / SANITY`: `CadEmailWorkspace.tsx` remains `2914` lines and ends with `export const CadEmailWorkspace = EmailCaddyWorkspace;`. Broad no-live scan hits were inert boundary strings and forbidden-key lists only; narrower executable-call/import scan for fetch/XHR/WebSocket/EventSource/browser storage/Dexie/fs/provider SDK imports exited `1` with no output.
- `MEMORY`: Added one concise process lesson to `docs/codex-experience-memory.md`: callable injected test doubles are not enforceably safe; source-gated executable contracts should validate metadata/request shape and stay no-call until a reviewed execution boundary exists. ## TC V3 Integrations UI Feedback Residual - 2026-06-13 23:20 EDT / 2026-06-14 03:20 UTC

Status: `USER UI REQUIREMENTS RECORDED / IMPLEMENTATION NOT STARTED`. This is a new UI residual, not accepted source work.

- `REFERENCE SCREENSHOTS`: `/Users/brdavies/Desktop/Screenshot 2026-06-13 at 10.50.55 PM.png`, `/Users/brdavies/Desktop/Screenshot 2026-06-13 at 10.52.57 PM.png`, and `/Users/brdavies/Desktop/Screenshot 2026-06-13 at 10.53.14 PM.png`.
- `PRODUCT PRINCIPLE`: integrations should expand real capability, not decorative explanation. Working mantra for the next UI wave: add what is necessary, not what merely looks good.
- `IMAGE 1 ISSUE`: the current integrations surface has too much explanatory copy, status decoration, bubbles/badges, preview panels, and passive placeholder text. The UI should be cleaner and less confusing, especially while capability is gated or not ready to test.
- `IMAGE 2 VS IMAGE 3`: Image 2 has the preferred simpler integration interaction model: ordered installed/catalog/history layout, clear rows, compact settings actions, and direct credential input. Image 3 has a potentially appealing theme/color direction, but the expanded placeholder/card text is too heavy. Keep the simpler Image 2 interaction model while allowing the Image 3 palette direction only when it fits the selected theme.
- `DEFAULT STYLE REQUIREMENT`: the Image 2 style/coloring should be treated as the likely default ThreatCaddy integrations style across all integrations, unless the user chooses a different app theme; integration surfaces should theme consistently with that choice.
- `FORM REQUIREMENT`: if an integration only needs an API key, show only the API key input plus required save/cancel actions. If additional fields are truly required for that provider, include them; otherwise do not add future-facing placeholder fields or descriptive panels.
- `ORDERING REQUIREMENT`: old integrations must not be stuffed away in a separate area while new integrations are ordered. All integrations should use the same standard presentation, ordering model, and settings pattern unless there is a functional reason to separate them.
- `PLACEHOLDER REQUIREMENT`: future capability placeholders should be hidden, collapsed, or scoped to a testable readiness state instead of adding visible bubbles/text/noise. Gated features can be surfaced when there is an actionable path to configure or test them.
- `NEXT UI WAVE`: before implementation, run a source-scope pass to identify the exact integrations UI files and define a narrow write set for standardizing the integration list/card/settings pattern. Prioritize removing fluff, unifying old/new integration ordering, and simplifying required credential forms before adding new capability copy.

## TC V3 Runtime Dry-Run Contract Root Enablement Source Gate Closeout - 2026-06-14 21:35 EDT / 2026-06-15 01:35 UTC

Messaging invocation now requires exact trusted accepted dry-run harness metadata at the consuming boundary. Credential preflight/persistence roots require trusted roots before semantic reads. Generic credential-store metadata IDs now use a separate sanitizer from credential-handle IDs so `provider-oauth:`, `vault:`, `local-bridge:`, URLs, and other scheme-shaped values are valid only where explicitly allowed as credential handle references.
